title: Lastrea trichotoma
taxonomy: Lastrea trichotoma is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a part of the plant kingdom. The family Athyriaceae is a group of vascular plants that are primarily found in Asia and the Pacific Islands. This fern is typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, often growing in shaded areas with high humidity. The classification of Lastrea trichotoma places it within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida.

intro: Lastrea trichotoma, also known as Trichomanes trichomanoides, is a species of fern belonging to the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea trichotoma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Lastrea trichotoma is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
